# unifi_dns_policy (Resource)

Manage a UniFi DNS policy. The active type-specific attributes depend on `type`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
# Manage a DNS policy such as an A record override.
data "unifi_site" "main" {
  name = "Default"
}

resource "unifi_dns_policy" "printer" {
  site_id      = data.unifi_site.main.id
  type         = "A_RECORD"
  enabled      = true
  domain       = "printer.iot.internal"
  ipv4_address = "10.30.0.50"
  ttl_seconds  = 300
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `domain` (String)
- `enabled` (Boolean)
- `site_id` (String)
- `type` (String) DNS policy type. Supported values: `A_RECORD`, `AAAA_RECORD`, `CNAME_RECORD`, `MX_RECORD`, `TXT_RECORD`, `SRV_RECORD`, `FORWARD_DOMAIN`.

### Optional

- `ip_address` (String)
- `ipv4_address` (String)
- `ipv6_address` (String)
- `mail_server_domain` (String)
- `port` (Number)
- `priority` (Number)
- `protocol` (String)
- `server_domain` (String)
- `service` (String)
- `target_domain` (String)
- `text` (String)
- `ttl_seconds` (Number)
- `weight` (Number)

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import a DNS policy by site and policy identifier.
terraform import unifi_dns_policy.printer <site_id>/<dns_policy_id>
```
